     6→# Parses INI files, validates against schemas, outputs JSON and YAML.
     7→# Built using TDD: tests written first, then implementation to pass them.
     8→# =============================================================================

    10→function ConvertTo-TypedValue {
    11→    <#
    12→    .SYNOPSIS
    13→        Coerces a string value to its most appropriate PowerShell type.
    14→    .DESCRIPTION
    15→        Attempts to convert a raw INI string value into a boolean, integer,
    16→        double, or keeps it as a string. Quoted values are unquoted and forced
    17→        to remain strings, preventing "123" from becoming an integer.
    18→    #>
    19→    [CmdletBinding()]
    20→    [OutputType([object])]
    21→    param(
    22→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    23→        [AllowEmptyString()]
    24→        [string]$Value
    25→    )
    26→
    27→    # Empty string stays as-is
    28→    if ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($Value)) {
    29→        return [string]$Value
    30→    }
    31→
    32→    # Quoted values: strip quotes and return as string (no coercion)
    33→    if ($Value.StartsWith('"') -and $Value.EndsWith('"') -and $Value.Length -ge 2) {
    34→        return [string]$Value.Substring(1, $Value.Length - 2)
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    # Boolean coercion (case-insensitive)
    38→    [string]$lower = $Value.ToLower()
    39→    if ($lower -eq 'true' -or $lower -eq 'yes' -or $lower -eq 'on') {
    40→        return [bool]$true
    41→    }
    42→    if ($lower -eq 'false' -or $lower -eq 'no' -or $lower -eq 'off') {
    43→        return [bool]$false
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    # Integer coercion (must be pure digits, optionally with leading minus)
    47→    [int]$intResult = 0
    48→    if ([int]::TryParse($Value, [ref]$intResult)) {
    49→        # Verify it's a clean integer string (no decimals, no extra chars)
    50→        if ($Value -match '^-?\d+$') {
    51→            return [int]$intResult
    52→        }
    53→    }
    54→
    55→    # Double/float coercion
    56→    [double]$dblResult = 0.0
    57→    if ($Value -match '^-?\d+\.\d+$') {
    58→        if ([double]::TryParse($Value, [System.Globalization.NumberStyles]::Float,
    59→            [System.Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture, [ref]$dblResult)) {
    60→            return [double]$dblResult
    61→        }
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    # Default: return as string
    65→    return [string]$Value
    66→}

    68→function ConvertFrom-IniFile {
    69→    <#
    70→    .SYNOPSIS
    71→        Parses an INI file into a nested hashtable structure.
    72→    .DESCRIPTION
    73→        Reads an INI configuration file and returns a hashtable of hashtables.
    74→        Each section becomes a top-level key; keys before any section header
    75→        go under '_global'. Supports semicolon and hash comments, multi-line
    76→        values (continuation lines starting with whitespace), and optional
    77→        type coercion.
    78→    #>
    79→    [CmdletBinding()]
    80→    [OutputType([hashtable])]
    81→    param(
    82→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
    83→        [string]$Path,
    84→
    85→        [Parameter()]
    86→        [switch]$CoerceTypes
    87→    )
    88→
    89→    # Validate file exists
    90→    if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $Path)) {
    91→        throw "INI file does not exist: $Path"
    92→    }
    93→
    94→    [string[]]$lines = Get-Content -LiteralPath $Path -Encoding UTF8
    95→
    96→    # Result: ordered hashtable of section -> ordered hashtable of key -> value
    97→    [hashtable]$result = @{}
    98→    [string]$currentSection = '_global'
    99→    [string]$currentKey = ''
   100→    [string]$currentValue = ''
   101→    [bool]$inMultiLine = $false
   102→
   103→    # Helper to save the current key-value pair
   104→# We use a scriptblock instead of a nested function to avoid scope issues
   105→    [scriptblock]$saveCurrentPair = {
   106→        param([hashtable]$res, [string]$sec, [string]$key, [string]$val, [bool]$coerce)
   107→        if ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($key)) { return }
   108→        if (-not $res.ContainsKey($sec)) {
   109→            $res[$sec] = [ordered]@{}
   110→        }
   111→        [string]$trimmedVal = $val.TrimEnd()
   112→        if ($coerce) {
   113→            $res[$sec][$key] = ConvertTo-TypedValue -Value $trimmedVal
   114→        }
   115→        else {
   116→            $res[$sec][$key] = $trimmedVal
   117→        }
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    foreach ($rawLine in $lines) {
   121→        [string]$line = $rawLine
   122→
   123→        # Check for continuation lines (multi-line values)
   124→        # A continuation line starts with whitespace and we have a current key
   125→        if ($inMultiLine -and $line.Length -gt 0 -and ($line[0] -eq ' ' -or $line[0] -eq "`t")) {
   126→            # This is a continuation of the previous value
   127→            $currentValue = $currentValue + "`n" + $line.Trim()
   128→            continue
   129→        }
   130→
   131→        # If we were in a multi-line value but this line isn't a continuation, save
   132→        if ($inMultiLine) {
   133→            & $saveCurrentPair $result $currentSection $currentKey $currentValue ([bool]$CoerceTypes)
   134→            $currentKey = ''
   135→            $currentValue = ''
   136→            $inMultiLine = $false
   137→        }
   138→
   139→        # Skip empty lines
   140→        [string]$trimmed = $line.Trim()
   141→        if ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($trimmed)) {
   142→            continue
   143→        }
   144→
   145→        # Skip comment lines (lines starting with ; or #)
   146→        if ($trimmed[0] -eq ';' -or $trimmed[0] -eq '#') {
   147→            continue
   148→        }
   149→
   150→        # Section header: [section_name]
   151→        if ($trimmed -match '^\[(.+)\]$') {
   152→            $currentSection = $Matches[1].Trim()
   153→            if (-not $result.ContainsKey($currentSection)) {
   154→                $result[$currentSection] = [ordered]@{}
   155→            }
   156→            continue
   157→        }
   158→
   159→        # Key-value pair: key = value (split only on first '=')
   160→        [int]$eqIdx = $trimmed.IndexOf('=')
   161→        if ($eqIdx -ge 0) {
   162→            $currentKey = $trimmed.Substring(0, $eqIdx).Trim()
   163→            if ($eqIdx -lt $trimmed.Length - 1) {
   164→                $currentValue = $trimmed.Substring($eqIdx + 1).Trim()
   165→            }
   166→            else {
   167→                $currentValue = ''
   168→            }
   169→            $inMultiLine = $true
   170→            continue
   171→        }
   172→    }
   173→
   174→    # Save any trailing multi-line value
   175→    if ($inMultiLine -and -not [string]::IsNullOrEmpty($currentKey)) {
   176→        & $saveCurrentPair $result $currentSection $currentKey $currentValue ([bool]$CoerceTypes)
   177→    }
   178→
   179→    return $result
   180→}

   182→function Test-IniSchema {
   183→    <#
   184→    .SYNOPSIS
   185→        Validates a parsed INI config hashtable against a JSON schema.
   186→    .DESCRIPTION
   187→        Checks required keys exist and values match expected types.
   188→        Returns a PSCustomObject with IsValid (bool) and Errors (string[]).
   189→    #>
   190→    [CmdletBinding()]
   191→    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
   192→    param(
   193→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   194→        [hashtable]$Config,
   195→
   196→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   197→        [string]$SchemaPath
   198→    )
   199→
   200→    if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $SchemaPath)) {
   201→        throw "Schema file does not exist: $SchemaPath"
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    [string]$schemaJson = Get-Content -LiteralPath $SchemaPath -Raw -Encoding UTF8
   205→    [PSCustomObject]$schema = $schemaJson | ConvertFrom-Json
   206→
   207→    [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]$errors = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
   208→
   209→    # Iterate each section defined in the schema
   210→    foreach ($sectionProp in $schema.PSObject.Properties) {
   211→        [string]$sectionName = $sectionProp.Name
   212→        [PSCustomObject]$sectionSchema = $sectionProp.Value
   213→
   214→        # Check if the section exists in config
   215→        [bool]$sectionExists = $Config.ContainsKey($sectionName)
   216→
   217→        foreach ($keyProp in $sectionSchema.PSObject.Properties) {
   218→            [string]$keyName = $keyProp.Name
   219→            [PSCustomObject]$keySchema = $keyProp.Value
   220→
   221→            [string]$expectedType = [string]$keySchema.type
   222→            [bool]$isRequired = [bool]$keySchema.required
   223→
   224→            # Check if the key exists
   225→            [bool]$keyExists = $false
   226→            if ($sectionExists) {
   227→                [System.Collections.Specialized.OrderedDictionary]$sectionData = $Config[$sectionName]
   228→                $keyExists = $sectionData.Contains($keyName)
   229→            }
   230→
   231→            # Required key check
   232→            if ($isRequired -and -not $keyExists) {
   233→                $errors.Add("[$sectionName].$keyName is required but missing")
   234→                continue
   235→            }
   236→
   237→            # If key doesn't exist and isn't required, skip type check
   238→            if (-not $keyExists) {
   239→                continue
   240→            }
   241→
   242→            # Type validation
   243→            $value = $Config[$sectionName][$keyName]
   244→            [bool]$typeValid = Test-ValueType -Value $value -ExpectedType $expectedType
   245→
   246→            if (-not $typeValid) {
   247→                [string]$actualType = if ($null -eq $value) { 'null' } else { $value.GetType().Name }
   248→                $errors.Add("[$sectionName].$keyName expected type '$expectedType' but got '$actualType'")
   249→            }
   250→        }
   251→    }
   252→
   253→    return [PSCustomObject]@{
   254→        IsValid = ($errors.Count -eq 0)
   255→        Errors  = [string[]]$errors.ToArray()
   256→    }
   257→}

   259→function Test-ValueType {
   260→    <#
   261→    .SYNOPSIS
   262→        Checks whether a value matches an expected schema type name.
   263→    #>
   264→    [CmdletBinding()]
   265→    [OutputType([bool])]
   266→    param(
   267→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   268→        [AllowNull()]
   269→        [AllowEmptyString()]
   270→        [object]$Value,
   271→
   272→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   273→        [string]$ExpectedType
   274→    )
   275→
   276→    if ($null -eq $Value) {
   277→        return $false
   278→    }
   279→
   280→    switch ($ExpectedType) {
   281→        'string' {
   282→            return ($Value -is [string])
   283→        }
   284→        'integer' {
   285→            return ($Value -is [int] -or $Value -is [long])
   286→        }
   287→        'number' {
   288→            return ($Value -is [int] -or $Value -is [long] -or $Value -is [double] -or $Value -is [decimal])
   289→        }
   290→        'boolean' {

   292→        }
   293→        default {

   295→        }
   296→    }

   299→function ConvertTo-JsonConfig {
   300→    <#
   301→    .SYNOPSIS
   302→        Converts a parsed INI hashtable to a JSON string.
   303→    .DESCRIPTION
   304→        Produces a formatted JSON document preserving the section/key structure.
   305→        Optionally writes to a file.
   306→    #>
   307→    [CmdletBinding()]
   308→    [OutputType([string])]
   309→    param(
   310→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   311→        [hashtable]$Config,
   312→
   313→        [Parameter()]
   314→        [string]$OutputPath = ''
   315→    )
   316→
   317→    # Build an ordered structure for deterministic output
   318→    [ordered]$ordered = [ordered]@{}
   319→    foreach ($section in ($Config.Keys | Sort-Object)) {
   320→        [ordered]$sectionData = [ordered]@{}
   321→        foreach ($key in $Config[$section].Keys) {
   322→            $sectionData[$key] = $Config[$section][$key]
   323→        }
   324→        $ordered[$section] = $sectionData
   325→    }
   326→
   327→    [string]$json = $ordered | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10
   328→
   329→    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrEmpty($OutputPath)) {
   330→        $json | Set-Content -LiteralPath $OutputPath -Encoding UTF8 -NoNewline
   331→    }
   332→
   333→    return $json
   334→}

   336→function ConvertTo-YamlConfig {
   337→    <#
   338→    .SYNOPSIS
   339→        Converts a parsed INI hashtable to a YAML-formatted string.
   340→    .DESCRIPTION
   341→        Produces a simple YAML document. Strings containing special YAML
   342→        characters are double-quoted. Booleans and numbers are unquoted.
   343→        Optionally writes to a file.
   344→    #>
   345→    [CmdletBinding()]
   346→    [OutputType([string])]
   347→    param(
   348→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   349→        [hashtable]$Config,
   350→
   351→        [Parameter()]
   352→        [string]$OutputPath = ''
   353→    )
   354→
   355→    [System.Text.StringBuilder]$sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
   356→
   357→    [bool]$firstSection = $true
   358→    foreach ($section in ($Config.Keys | Sort-Object)) {
   359→        if (-not $firstSection) {
   360→            [void]$sb.AppendLine('')
   361→        }
   362→        $firstSection = $false
   363→        [void]$sb.AppendLine("${section}:")
   364→
   365→        foreach ($key in $Config[$section].Keys) {
   366→            $value = $Config[$section][$key]
   367→            [string]$yamlValue = Format-YamlValue -Value $value
   368→            [void]$sb.AppendLine("  ${key}: $yamlValue")
   369→        }
   370→    }
   371→
   372→    [string]$yaml = $sb.ToString()
   373→
   374→    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrEmpty($OutputPath)) {
   375→        $yaml | Set-Content -LiteralPath $OutputPath -Encoding UTF8 -NoNewline
   376→    }
   377→
   378→    return $yaml
   379→}
   380→
   381→function Format-YamlValue {
   382→    <#
   383→    .SYNOPSIS
   384→        Formats a single value for YAML output.
   385→    .DESCRIPTION
   386→        Booleans become true/false, numbers are unquoted, strings with
   387→        special characters get double-quoted, plain strings stay unquoted.
   388→    #>
   389→    [CmdletBinding()]
   390→    [OutputType([string])]
   391→    param(
   392→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   393→        [AllowNull()]
   394→        [AllowEmptyString()]
   395→        [object]$Value
   396→    )
   397→
   398→    if ($null -eq $Value) {
   399→        return 'null'
   400→    }
   401→
   402→    if ($Value -is [bool]) {
   403→        if ([bool]$Value) { return 'true' } else { return 'false' }
   404→    }
   405→
   406→    if ($Value -is [int] -or $Value -is [long] -or $Value -is [double] -or $Value -is [decimal]) {
   407→        return $Value.ToString([System.Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture)
   408→    }
   409→
   410→    # String value - check if quoting is needed
   411→    [string]$strVal = [string]$Value
   412→
   413→    if ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($strVal)) {
   414→        return '""'
   415→    }
   416→
   417→    # Multi-line strings use YAML literal block scalar
   418→    if ($strVal.Contains("`n")) {
   419→        [string]$indented = $strVal -replace "`n", "`n    "
   420→        return "|-`n    $indented"
   421→    }
   422→
   423→    # Quote strings containing YAML-special characters
   424→    [bool]$needsQuoting = $false
   425→    if ($strVal -match '[:#\[\]{}|>&*!?%@`,]') {
   426→        $needsQuoting = $true
   427→    }
   428→    # Quote if looks like a boolean or number in YAML
   429→    if ($strVal -match '^(true|false|yes|no|on|off|null|~)$') {
   430→        $needsQuoting = $true
   431→    }
   432→    if ($strVal -match '^\d') {
   433→        $needsQuoting = $true
   434→    }
   435→
   436→    if ($needsQuoting) {
   437→        # Escape existing double quotes and backslashes for YAML
   438→        [string]$escaped = $strVal -replace '\\', '\\' -replace '"', '\"'
   439→        return """$escaped"""
   440→    }
   441→
   442→    return $strVal
   443→}

   445→function Convert-ConfigFile {
   446→    <#
   447→    .SYNOPSIS
   448→        Full pipeline: parse INI, optionally validate, output JSON and YAML.
   449→    .DESCRIPTION
   450→        Orchestrates the full config migration workflow. Parses the INI file
   451→        with type coercion, validates against a schema if provided, and writes
   452→        JSON and YAML output files. Returns a result object with Success and
   453→        Errors properties.
   454→    #>
   455→    [CmdletBinding()]
   456→    [OutputType([PSCustomObject])]
   457→    param(
   458→        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
   459→        [string]$IniPath,
   460→
   461→        [Parameter()]
   462→        [string]$SchemaPath = '',
   463→
   464→        [Parameter()]
   465→        [string]$JsonOutputPath = '',
   466→
   467→        [Parameter()]
   468→        [string]$YamlOutputPath = ''
   469→    )
   470→
   471→    # Parse the INI file with type coercion
   472→    [hashtable]$config = ConvertFrom-IniFile -Path $IniPath -CoerceTypes
   473→
   474→    # Validate against schema if provided
   475→    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrEmpty($SchemaPath)) {
   476→        [PSCustomObject]$validation = Test-IniSchema -Config $config -SchemaPath $SchemaPath
   477→
   478→        if (-not $validation.IsValid) {
   479→            return [PSCustomObject]@{
   480→                Success = [bool]$false
   481→                Config  = $config
   482→                Errors  = [string[]]$validation.Errors

   487→    # Generate outputs
   488→    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrEmpty($JsonOutputPath)) {

   491→
   492→    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrEmpty($YamlOutputPath)) {

   494→    }
   495→
   496→    return [PSCustomObject]@{
   497→        Success = [bool]$true
   498→        Config  = $config
   499→        Errors  = [string[]]@()
   500→    }
   501→}

   503→# Export public functions
   504→Export-ModuleMember -Function @(

   506→    'ConvertTo-TypedValue'
   507→    'Test-IniSchema'
   508→    'ConvertTo-JsonConfig'
   509→    'ConvertTo-YamlConfig'
   510→    'Convert-ConfigFile'
   511→)
